characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The founders of the Galactic Protectorate. Arch-Enemy: To Mars. Outside of the war, Earth regards Mars as eitherThe Rivalorfriendly rivalsdepending on how disgruntled they are. Big Good: Earth helps other planets fight off invasions, promotes peace, and tries to give materials to planets that are struggling. Blue Is Heroic: Earth is considered to be aBig Good, and the backgrounds on Earth usually have a prominent blue color scheme. The Galactic Protectorate also heavily uses blue. Earth Is the Center of the Universe: As Dodgers in the protagonist of the show, this is a given. Earth is the hub of the Galactic Protectorate, a powerful organization that promotes peace between planets. Earth also harbors a lot of different alien species compared to other planets.

characters.name: Captain Duck Dodgers
characters.description: The titular "hero" of the series, Dodgers is a captain in the protectorate who was revived by I.Q. Hi after being frozen for four and a half centuries. Despite I.Q.'s hopes for Dodgers to be a useful asset, he's at best a wild card for the protectorate; Generally Dodgers ishighlyincompetent, unprofessional,self absorbed, and amoral but hedoeshave the occasional moment ofgenuine competence. Occasional being the key word. Absurd Phobia: Dodgers fears pillow shams, home decor, fruit, vegetables, exercising, taxes, and mathematics. These are lies on Dodgers part, however. Dodgers is seen performing mathematical equations in "The Queen is Wild" and exercising whenever heactuallytakes part in a mission. Adaptational Badass: Duck Dodgers in this show has had more victories, combat skill, and even moments of competence compared to the original shorts.Adaptational Dumbass: While Dodgers in the original short did tend to miss a few vital details, he wasn't actually stupid. In this show, he's a certifiedCloud Cuckoolanderwho, depending on the episode, swings between being aGenius Ditzand a straight-up ditz.Adaptational Heroism:However, despite the traditional Daffy Duck selfishness and narcissism, he does have a nobler side to his character. His Jerkass moments stem from his tendency to get carried away by his base desires, or forgetting that he's not the only actual person in the universe. On the occasions when he manages to overcome these tendencies, he can be downright heroic. Adaptational Dumbass: While Dodgers in the original short did tend to miss a few vital details, he wasn't actually stupid. In this show, he's a certifiedCloud Cuckoolanderwho, depending on the episode, swings between being aGenius Ditzand a straight-up ditz. Adaptational Heroism:However, despite the traditional Daffy Duck selfishness and narcissism, he does have a nobler side to his character. His Jerkass moments stem from his tendency to get carried away by his base desires, or forgetting that he's n

characters.name: The Eager Young Space Cadet
characters.description: Captain Duck Dodgers' loyal right hand pig andthe only other crewmen of the ship. Despite being of lower rank, The Cadet is more often than not thefarmore competent and hardworking of the two, as well the more noble. Though he is for most part, very loyal to his captain, The Cadet is far from unaware of Dodgers' flaws and does enjoy poking fun at the duck from time to time. Almighty Janitor: In "Win, Lose, or Duck", he says he was tricked into applying for the Academy by an advert. Despite being more competent than his captain, he doesn't make much, and once went 4 years without a vacation. Attractive Bent-Gender: Both Duck Dodgers and X-2 think so. "She" also speaks with a southern accent. Ax-Crazy: While under the effects of moognesium in "Pig of Action". Beleaguered Assistant: To Captain Dodgers, who would likely not be able to function, if not for the Cadet having to take care of him.

characters.name: Dr. Ignatius Q "I.Q." Hi
characters.description: Also known as the "Secretary of the Stratosphere", I.Q. is the scientist who revived Duck Dodgers from his frozen stasis, afterwards recruiting the duck into The Protectorate. He also serves as Dodgers' and some of The Protectorate'sde factoboss. Big Good: He's the one that unfroze Duck Dodgers, believing the duck to be a hero that could help protect people. He is one of the more proactive leaders of the Protectorate (described as the "Secretary of the Stratosphere" in season 2's finale) and usually is the one giving mission statements to Dodgers and the Cadet. Ditzy Genius: Dr. Hi has his moments, like when he ventured into Dodgers' mind to get aMacGuffinhidden within it and ends up in a bait shop within Dodgers' mind (It Makes Sense in Context... kind of), he insists on buying something that serves no practical purpose in the bait shop that only exists within Dodgers' mind and therefore, would cease to exist once they leave. Dr. Genericius: He's a doctor, and his first name ends with "-ius". Enraged by Idiocy: Downplayed, and targeted. While he's only a bit annoyed when someone else is acting stupid, he is noticeably aggravated whenever Dodgers displays any form of incompetence. This is probably due to him being the one to vouch for Dodgers being a competent captain.

characters.name: Dr. Psy-Q Hi
characters.description: A psychoanalyst working in a Psychiatric Medical Center, and I.Q.'s brother. Fat and Skinny: He and I.Q. Hi make up this type of duo. Meaningful Name: "Psy-Q" sounds similar to "psycho", matching his profession as a psychoanalyst. Remember the New Guy?: I.Q.'s brother, a figure influential enough to end up at treaty tables, and he only appears in season 2's finale. Also, as much as I.Q. is less known than a Looney Tune like Elmer, Psy-Q is even more forgettable. The Shrink: He's a psychoanalyst by trade. In his debut episode, hetriesto help Dodgers alleviate his guilt in falsely accusing the Cadet of sabotage, to little success.

characters.name: Captain Star Johnson
characters.description: A fellow Captain of The Protectorate and rival to Duck Dodgers. The Ace: He's considered to be one of the best captains in the Protectorate. Ace Pilot: Shows his skills as a pilot in the season 2 finale by single-handedly taking out several martian ships. Blue Is Heroic: His uniform and allegiance evokes this. Casanova Wannabe: Most of his appearances involve him trying to seduce another captain or cadet. While the ladies initially look interested, something happens (usually involving Dodgers) that immediately ceases their interest.

characters.name: Captain Aurora Soleil Jean
characters.description: A competent Captain of the Protectorate. Both Duck Dodgers and Star Johnson try to impress her. The Ace: Blew the previous ace—Captain Star Johnson—out of the water with her dedication to justice. Action Girl: Noted for being one of the most capable cadets in the Protectorate. So capable that she was promoted to captain in the captain award ceremony. Captain Space, Defender of Earth!: Out of every captain seen on the show, she has the most achievements and was awarded "Captain of the Year". Head-Turning Beauty: Exaggerated for comedic effect. Her beauty is so enchanting that not only does Star Johnson, Dodgers, and the Cadet turn to look at her whenever she's around, she begins to glow and the background changes to flowers and glitter.

characters.name: Emily Dickinson Jones
characters.description: The instructor for those wanting to enter the Galactic Protectorate. Drill Sergeant Nasty: A classic depiction, with bulging veins and all. Gender-Blender Name: In the 24½th century, gender-based naming has pretty much disappeared, so it's normal for a man to go by Emily. Named After Somebody Famous: In-Universe. His mother named him after American poet Emily Dickinson. Promotion, Not Punishment: When Dodgers is demoted from captain and fails his captain training, he breaks into Jones' room and rewrites all of his scores so he passes. Instead of being pissed that Dodgers broke the rules, Jones is ecstatic that he managed to change his scores without alerting either Jones or the security system. As such, Dodgers is allowed to become captain again.

characters.name: Captain Long
characters.description: Captain Space, Defender of Earth!: He's one of the Protectorate's many captains. His ship is tasked with patrolling the outer boundaries of Earth's sphere of influence. Shout-Out: He's a shoutout toStar Trek'sCaptain Kirk due to having two characters inspired by Spock and Lieutenant Nyota O'Uhura on his ship.

characters.name: Lieutenant O'Hara
characters.description: A crewmember of Captain Long's ship. Communications Officer: She communicates back and forth between other Protectorate ships. Shout-Out: ToStar Trek'sLieutenant Nyota O'Uhura.

characters.name: Agent Nikki Yoshimi
characters.description: An agent featured in "The Spy Who Didn't Love Me". AnotherLove Interestof Dodgers. Action Girl: The President of Space assigned her to organize and co-lead rebels to liberate the planet Andromeda 6 from Mars. Head-Turning Beauty: Like every girl, Dodgers reacted this way. Oblivious to Love: She isn't aware Dodgers has a crush on her. At the end of the episode, she falls in love with a rebel called Dax instead.

characters.name: Cassiopeia
characters.description: A popstar that Dodgers falls head over heels for. Everyone Loves Blondes: Everyone in the club seems to like her, or at least enjoy her performance. Probably because of her music and face, not her hair. Expy: If this wasn't Warner Bros, you could say she's Jem.see for yourself. Head-Turning Beauty: What Dodgers thinks when he first sees her. Magic Idol Singer: While it's not clarified if she's an idol singer, she must be magical because she floats.

characters.name: Miss Betsy
characters.description: A southern belle that Dodgers falls for. She owns a restaurant. Damsel in Distress: In the song about Dodgers and his fight to save Vacationworld from Nasty Canasta, she becomes a damsel when she's kidnapped by the outlaw. Everyone Loves Blondes: Dodgers loves her. Southern Belle: Pretty, and speaks with a southern accent.

characters.name: Theresa
characters.description: A Spanish woman that Dodgers likes. Damsel in Distress: She gets locked in jail by Hilgago.... Damsel out of Distress: But she breaks out of jail with the help of the Cadet. Raven Hair, Ivory Skin: She doesn't look like the traditional mental image Americans have of Hispanics, but there are fair skinned Spaniards. However, her name would usually be spelled as 'Teresa' in Spanish. Spicy Latina: She's a Spaniard, but she is still very moody and hot-blooded, as well as attractive.

characters.name: Lady Winifred Chanticleer
characters.description: A famous diva that Duck Dodgers has to deal with. Ambiguously Human: She looks human but has pointy ears. The Bully: She goes out of her way to abuse Dodgers, even though he never stops treating her with courtesy. Rich Bitch: She's part of the upper crust and a jerk to boot. Sassy Black Woman: She's a mean, moody black woman who spends most of her time yelling at people.

characters.name: CommanderX-2
characters.description: The primary commander of The Martian forces and Duck Dodgers' biggest rival. Abusive Parents: X-2's father used to dangle him from the balcony as a child for publicity. Ace Pilot: He's considered to be Mars' unbeatable champion pilot—and technically still is, as Dodgers only beat him through cheating and dumb luck. Adaptation Personality Change:Justified, as Marvin is playing the character X-2 rather than acting as himself.Marvin the Martian ispolite, soft-spoken,The Ever-Calm, and has aBlue-and-Orange Morality. He can also be rather oblivious and innocently insensitive. Commander X-2 is also polite, but expressive and aDeadpan Snarkerwith the tendency to be passive-aggressive and petty. He also has a more understandable moral compass compared to Marvin, where Marvin wants to destroy Earth because it gets in the middle of his view of Venus while Commander X-2 wants to destroy Earth so Mars can remain on top.They have different occupations and goals: Marvin is a scientist first, usually prioritizing research over everything else, and rarely acts under someone else's orders. Commander X-2, on the other hand, is a commander first and foremost, and doesn't care much about scientific pursuits despite being adept at it. He also directly answers to Queen Tyr'ahnee. Marvin the Martian ispolite, soft-spoken,The Ever-Calm, and has aBlue-and-Orange Morality. He can also be rather oblivious and innocently insensitive. Commander X-2 is also polite, but expressive and aDeadpan Snarkerwith the tendency to be passive-aggressive and petty. He also has a more understandable moral compass compared to Marvin, where Marvin wants to destroy Earth because it gets in the middle of his view of Venus while Commander X-2 wants to destroy Earth so Mars can remain on top.

characters.name: K-9
characters.description: Commander X-2's Martian dog. Action Pet: Subverted. He's willing to put his life on the line for X-2, but he wasn't trained for fighting. Amusing Injuries: Gets hurt a lot trying to protect X-2. The Dog Is an Alien: He's a Martian Dog. Undying Loyalty: To X-2.

characters.name: Queen Tyr'ahnee
characters.description: Ace Pilot: Downplayed. While not directly controlling the ships, she is excellent at predicting her enemies' movements and shooting them down before they can react. Action Girl/Dark Action Girl: While she generally leaves combat to her military and ended up serving as aDamsel in Distressat least once, she is shown to be a formidable fighter in in both hand-to-hand and vehicular combat from time to time. Affably Evil: She's aReasonable Authority Figureto Mars (her obsession with Dodgers notwithstanding) and is on friendly terms with I.Q. Hi despite being enemies. Beautiful Singing Voice: Sings a beautiful rendition of "Blues in the Night", which impresses most of the audience. Benevolent Boss: She treats her subordinates well and is forgiving to X-2 despite his repeated failures. The only time she's gotten mad with him was when she found he wasresponsiblefor the moment where Dodgers became their greatest enemy, or when she wanted to propose to Dodgers and got angry at X-2 for hurting him—even though she told him to kidnap Dodgers in the first place and didn't bother explaining that she wanted to marry Dodgers.

characters.name: Martian Centurions
characters.description: An army of robots and the backbone of The Martian Military. Generally they are just an army of faceless minions, but they are shown to be capable independent thought, at least to an extent. One or more of them are often used when the show needs more Martian characters to play off of than just X-2 and Queen Tyr'ahnee. Affably Evil: Being jerks with to X-2 aside, they are shown to bePunch Clock Villainswho are nice to be around. Cyber Cyclops: The centurions under X-2's command each have one eye. Friendly Enemies: Similar to their respective superiors, the Centurions are shown to be this with The Cadet on at least one occasion. Jerkass to One: They don't like their commander and mock him behind his back. They are otherwise polite.

characters.name: Counselor Dish
characters.description: Z-9's right-hand officer. Curb-Stomp Battle: Is on both ends in one episode. First, she easily beats several of Marvin the Martian's minions in hand-to-hand combat. Later, she's quickly beaten by Tyr'ahnee during the climax. Dark Action Girl: She isn't afraid to get her hands dirty, and can easily defeat several of Marvin's bird henchmen. However, she is no match for Queen Tyr'ahnee. The Dragon: She's Z-9 right-hand. Fem Bot: She's a robot but identifies as female and even has a feminine design with noticeable breasts and make-up.

characters.name: Drake Darkstar
characters.description: A criminal mastermind that looks like Duck Dodgers. Criminal Doppelgänger: He looks exactly like Duck Dodgers with the exception of his head feathers and evil smile. He also has a similar alliterative name! Even Evil Has Standards: Even he remarks that Dodgers selling the Cadet's sister to the sausage factory is cold. Evil Brit: He has a British accent. Expy: Of Hannibal Lecter. He has the same demeanor as him as well as the same cannibalistic tendencies.

characters.name: Dr. Maniac
characters.description: A scientist (sort of) helping the Martians learn more about cyberorganic technology. Companion Cube: Talks to his keys like they're sentient. Mad Scientist: Qualifies as one, but Queen Tyr'ahnee notes that he's known for being a bad scientist overall. His debut episode reinforces this. Quack Doctor: Has the title, but in reality, he's not a real doctor—he's a circus clown.

characters.name: Dr. Woe
characters.description: An evil scientist trying to take over Mars. Arch-Enemy: Commander X-2 and Dr. Woe claim to be each other's archenemies in "Enemy Yours", to humorous effect. Commander X-2 eventually loses this view of him and considers Dodgers to be his archenemy. Evil Genius: Good at crafting inventions for less than savory purposes. Evil Versus Evil: He and Commander X-2 have been enemies for a long time, but neither really has a moral high ground, considering X-2 is a warmonger who's willing to destroy or enslave planets while Woe is a megalomaniac who wants to seize control over Mars. Morally Ambiguous Doctorate: As his name, Dr. Woe, implies. He's not above threatening to kill Queen Tyr'ahnee.

characters.name: The Fudd
characters.description: An evil alien with the power to change all sentient beings into similar-looking beings through a disease called "The Fudd". The Dreaded: He can spread an extremely contagious virus that forces people into aHive. Apparently, he's spread the Fudd more than once. However, it's been so long since he last appeared that when the Fudd infects a Protectorate ship and torpedoes it to Mars, Queen Tyr'ahnee dismisses Dr. Hi's warnings of the Fudd and assumes it's an attack made by Earth. The Hive: Elmer Fudd infects people with the titular disease and assimilates them into a hivemind with him calling the shots. The victims are completely unaware of what's happening until they are uninfected. Omnicidal Maniac: Turns out to be one completely by accident. He doesn't understand that sending everyone to invade the sun will kill them, nor that there is practically no benefit to invading the sun in the first place. Parody: Of the Flood inHaloand the Borg inStar Trek.

characters.name: Hella, Ramona and Angelica Sin
characters.description: Affably Evil: They're very cordial withThe Cadet, laughing at his humorous stories, charmed by him, while being far more icy towardsDodgers. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead:Or in their case: Black, black and green. Beauty Is Bad: Hella is more cute, but Ramona and Angelica are definitely beautiful. Dracula: They are parodies of Dracula's brides. Evil Sounds Deep: Ramona and Angelica, especially Angelica. Seethis video.

characters.name: Long John Silver the 23rd
characters.description: The leader of a group of space pirates. He debuts in "Shiver Me Dodgers". Affably Evil: Friendly, even when he's threatening someone's life. Beard of Evil: He sports a long, red beard. Public Domain Character: Of Long John Silver fromTreasure Island(a fact actuallylampshadedin the episode). Veers a bit intoExpyterritory. Space Pirates: He's the leader of a group of space pirates—with a large amount of treasure to boot.

characters.name: Magnificent Rogue
characters.description: A narcissistic celebrity. Face of an Angel, Mind of a Demon: Flowing golden locks, big blue eyes, a nice face, and a sculpted body. He uses his appearance to further his goals, which usually causes destruction in the pursuit of sating his own desires. Human Aliens: Implied. He looks human, but doesn't recognize Earth when he sees it. It's All About Me: Only cares about himself, and will pretend to be nice in order to get what he wants. Lovable Rogue:Deconstruction. He has the classic charm of a rogue from his appearance to his prestige, but his narcissistic, unpleasant personality and selfish desires eventually infuriate his fans enough to have them try and arrest him. Despite this, people who are blinded by celebrity culture overlook his worst aspects, even when his crimes involve flooding Earth.

characters.name: Sinestro
characters.description: The main enemy of the Green Lantern Corps, who schemes to wipe out the universe and remake it in his own image. Knight of Cerebus: Subverted. He initially looks like he'll be played as a serious villain, but when Dodgers meets him, he proves just as goofy as every other character in the show. Mecha-Mooks: As the cartoon predated the existence of the Sinestro Corps, Sinestro's underlings are a bunch of robot drones that vaguely resemble him. Names to Run Away from Really Fast: Sinestro is obviously an intimidating name and he looks to be a serious villain at first. Satanic Archetype: Dodgers outright points out to Sinestro that he looks like the Devil.

characters.name: K'Chutha Sa'am
characters.description: The leader of the Klunkins. For tropes related to Sam in general, seeYosemite Sam. The following are exclusive to this reincarnation of his. Asskicking Leads to Leadership: He's the leader of the Klunkins because he was able to beat everyone else to the position. Parody: OfStar Trek'sKlingons. Proud Warrior Race Guy: As a transparent homage to the Klingons, this is to be expected. He doesn't believe in surrendering or taking prisoners.

characters.name: Happy Cat
characters.description: A Japanese mascot brand. Cadet bought one of their alarm clocks as a Christmas gift for Dodgers and it never lets him rest. Ambiguously Evil:Played With. It's only antagonistic in the sense that it continually tries to get Dodgers out of bed when he's trying to be lazy. However, it does occasionally make oddly sinister comments.Happy Cat:Hahaha!Very funny, Lazy Duck. But you can never defeat me.NEVER! Ambiguously Sentient Object: While it seemed annoying at first, it suddenly calls Dodgers "Lazy Duck" in its first alarm clock episode. All ambiguity is thrown out the window in later episodes. Ascended Extra: In reference to its voice actor, it takes the role of villain in a parody ofSamurai Jack Evil Laugh: It makes a very sinister sounding laugh that's practically its catchphrase.

characters.name: Samantha de la Tortura
characters.description: A parody of the Bride of Frankenstein created by Duck Dodgers. Meaningful Name: Her title — de la Tortura — is Spanish for "of torture", which is what happened to create her. Parody: Of Frankenstein's monster's wife. She has the exact same name and attitude as the book version.

characters.name: Soren the New Cadet
characters.description: A woman who "replaces" Porky as the new cadet. Has an obsession towards Dodgers (who hates her). In Til Doom Do Us Part, she joins the Villain Union. Ax-Crazy: The moment Dodgers says he's not interested in a relationship, she claims he's like all the other men she stalked andattempts to kill him. Beauty Is Bad: Soren is a very beautiful woman... and anAx-CrazyStalker with a Crushwho's holding The Cadet captive, just so she could steal his job and get closer to Dodgers. Gender-Blender Name: Soren is usually a man's name. Stalker with a Crush: After Dodgers tries to hide from her, he chances upon her room, which contains multitudes of his photos—including those of private moments.

characters.name: Alien Hunter
characters.description: A hunter that wants to kill Commander X-2.For anything related to his main series shorts, seeLooney Tunes Major Characters. Cruel Coyotes:He is revealed at the end of "K-9 Quarry" to be none other than Wile E. Coyote, in perhaps one of his most villainous portrayals in theLooney Tunesfranchise. Dramatic Unmask: After he's defeated, he gets up from the himself-shaped hole in the ground and removes his helmet, revealing himself to beWile E. Coyote. Five-Second Foreshadowing: His menacing demeanor, his guttural noises, the fact that he has captured a fewHanna-Barberacharacters and the fact that he is targeting X-2 throughout "K-9 Quarry" lead us to believe that he's just a generic sci-fi villain with an unremarkable true identity, but then near the end, he's knocked off a cliff, and evenbriefly glances at the screen, in a similar fashion to a certain coyote. As it turns out,heisWile E. Coyote himself. Hoist by His Own Petard: His rolling log trap that ends up catching K-9 instead of X-2 ultimately leads to his defeat when the dog comes to his master's rescue by jumping off the logs and leaving them to knock him off the cliff he cornered X-2 at.

characters.name: Green Lantern Corps
characters.description: An intergalactic police force armed with rings that enable them to fly and create light constructs of whatever they can think of. Dodgers gets mixed up with them because of a laundromat mix-up with Hal Jordan giving him Jordan's uniform and ring. Adapted Out: Hal Jordan is the main human member of the Corps focused on and cameos are made by John Stewart and Guy Gardner, but Kyle Rayner (the only other human Green Lantern Corpsman who existed at the time the episode aired) doesn't appear at all. The Cameo: The majority of the Green Lanterns have no dialogue and are only shown very briefly in crowd shots. Crossover: The whole purpose of their episode is having a Looney Tunes property cross over with one of DC Comics. Intelligible Unintelligible: Unlike his comic counterpart, Ch'p is depicted as communicating through squirrel-like chittering, which the other Lanterns have no problem understanding (how much of this is because of their rings translating any language isn't made clear).

characters.name: Manobrain
characters.description: I.Q.'s old college friend turned into a criminal mastermind. Laser-Guided Amnesia: Frequently wipes peoples memories, sometimes at request. For example: after I.Q. walks into the President of Outer Space's closet and sees him wearing his wife's clothes, I.Q. asks to have his memory wiped of the event. Manobrain gladly erases it. Octopoid Aliens: An alien with an octopus-inspired design. Shout-Out: To D&Dillithids. Both are cephalopod aliens with large, portruding brains and psychic powers used to fly and manipulate other people's minds. Thankfully, Manobrain doesn't seem to have the same unsavory diet. We Used to Be Friends: Manobrain and I.Q. used to be friends and create inventions together. However, after the weight-loss pill they invented went missing, they blamed each other for its loss and a bitter rivalry began. In "A Lame Duck Mind", they manage to rekindle their friendship and remake the weight loss pill together, but history repeats when it goes missing again.

characters.name: The President of Outer Space
characters.description: The president of all known worlds allied with the Galactic Protectorate. An incredibly important figure in intergalactic politics. Behind Every Great Man: It's implied that his wife is the one who does most of the thinking. Caught in Embarrassing Act: In "A Lame Duck Mind", the President of Space accidentally locks himself inside his bedroom closet. While he's in there, he entertains himself by dressing like his wife and doing a mocking impression of her, but then Dodgers, the Cadet, I.Q. Hi, Manobrain, and the President's wife manage to unlock the closet door, and see him. The President is scared to see his wife giving him aDeath Glare, while I.Q. Hi asks Manobrain to use his psychic powers tomake him forget seeing that, which he does. Distressed Dude: He once gets trapped in his own closet and needs to be broken out by the Galactic Protectorate. Our Presidents Are Different: He's the elected president of an entire galaxy, and he is implied to be more of symbol of peace and authority than a leader.
